title: lindsaea walkerae
intro: Lindsaea walkerae, also known as the Walking Fern, is a rare and endangered species of fern found in the tropical regions of Asia, specifically in India and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Lindsaea walkerae include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and immunomodulator. Herbal preparations of Lindsaea walkerae typically involve drying and powdering the plant, which can then be used in teas, infusions, or as a topical application for its medicinal properties.
